provider_setup_meta.summary: Mailgun blocked — Resend interim Phase 2 paid + fallback pool; SES operational primary (sandbox until production)

providers.name: HubSpot
constraints: All paid users + company email on signup
providers.notes: Phase 3 terminal — paid CRM + company email. 1M contacts.

account_setup.notes: HubSpot account configured for Phase 3 terminal CRM.

providers.name: Beehiiv
constraints: No Send API on free tier
providers.upgrade_trigger: Contacts > 2,000 — prune users by graduated readiness or time-on-list; fallback pool or Phase 2
providers.notes: Phase 1 primary — nurture on Beehiiv while list headroom exists; track readiness milestones until upgrade or contact prune.

account_setup.notes: API key created — Phase 1 newsletter and list management.

mcp.cursor_steps: Settings → MCP → Add server → paste connector URL → OAuth in browser

providers.name: Brevo
constraints: Shared with CS agent transactional sends (PLAN.md)
providers.upgrade_trigger: Pool aggregate > 80% — any member may absorb overflow from any phase
providers.notes: Fallback pool member — interchangeable with other pool providers. Used when primary provider hits cap or errors.

paid_tier.name: Starter
paid_tier.notes: Removes free-tier 300/day and 2,000 contact caps. Replaces OmniSend for paid lifecycle; keep Mailgun/EmailOctopus on free tiers.

account_setup.notes: API key created (REST + transactional) — 300 emails/day; PH interim and CS agent. MCP-capable key for Cursor.

mcp.cursor_steps: Settings → MCP → url https://mcp.brevo.com/v1/brevo/mcp · Authorization: Bearer ${BREVO_MCP_TOKEN} (MCP token from Brevo → SMTP & API → API Keys)

providers.name: Mailgun
providers.notes: Replaced by Resend for Phase 2 paid until Mailgun access is restored.

account_setup.next_action: Use Resend for paid lifecycle; revisit Mailgun when access is restored.

providers.name: OmniSend
constraints: 250 contact cap below 500 subs year-end goal
providers.upgrade_trigger: Pool aggregate > 80% — any member may absorb overflow from any phase
providers.notes: Fallback pool member — interchangeable with other pool providers. Used when primary provider hits cap or errors.

account_setup.notes: OmniSend account created; domain verification in progress — 500 free emails/mo.
account_setup.next_action: Complete domain DNS verification in OmniSend.

providers.name: MailerLite
constraints: Contact cap binds before send cap
providers.upgrade_trigger: Pool aggregate > 80% — any member may absorb overflow from any phase
providers.notes: Fallback pool member — interchangeable with other pool providers. Used when primary provider hits cap or errors.

account_setup.notes: MailerLite account exists but login needs recovery.
account_setup.next_action: Recover MailerLite account login before using fallback pool capacity.

providers.name: EmailOctopus
providers.upgrade_trigger: Contacts > 2,000 — fallback pool (MailerLite, OmniSend, or Brevo) or Phase 3 transfer
providers.notes: Phase 2 (no upgrade) — 3–4 conversion emails; then Phase 3 perpetual free.

account_setup.notes: API key created — 2,500 subscribers · 10,000 emails/mo (Phase 2 conversion).

providers.name: Resend
constraints: Also fallback pool member — not for legal/outage (use SES)
providers.upgrade_trigger: Paid + fallback daily > 80 — spill to Brevo or Loops
providers.notes: Phase 2 paid primary (interim) + fallback pool. Paid Zen welcome, cancelled win-back. API sends via RESEND_API_KEY.

account_setup.notes: API key created — Phase 2 paid interim (replaces Mailgun) and lifecycle fallback overflow.

providers.name: Loops
constraints: Early Stage free — Powered by Loops footer
Marketing / product-help fallback only — not legal or outage
providers.notes: Lifecycle fallback pool member for nurture overflow when Beehiiv/EmailOctopus at cap.

account_setup.notes: Loops configured — lifecycle marketing fallback (not operational).

providers.name: Amazon SES
constraints: Sandbox: 200 emails/24h until production access approved
providers.notes: Primary operational lane (legal, privacy, outage). See docs/OPERATIONAL_EMAIL_RUNBOOK.md § SES production.

account_setup.notes: Amazon SES configured — 200 emails/day in sandbox until production access.
account_setup.next_action: Request AWS SES production access (see docs/OPERATIONAL_EMAIL_RUNBOOK.md § SES production).

provider_candidates.name: Sendy
provider_candidates.notes: Self-hosted — not SaaS free tier; evaluate separately

buckets.label: New users
buckets.definition: First day of engagement ever in the app

buckets.label: Current users
buckets.definition: Active today and at least one other day in the prior 6 days

buckets.label: Reactivated users
buckets.definition: First day back after 7–29 days away

buckets.label: Resurrected users
buckets.definition: First day back after 30+ days away

buckets.label: At-risk WAU
buckets.definition: Inactive today, active on at least one of the prior 6 days

buckets.label: At-risk MAU
buckets.definition: Inactive today and prior 6 days; active 7–29 days ago

buckets.label: Dead users
buckets.definition: No activity in the last 30 days

routing_rules.label: Fallback pool
routing_rules.trigger: Any primary provider (Beehiiv, EmailOctopus, Mailgun) > 80% cap
routing_rules.notes: Fallback pool — used only when a primary provider hits a cap or errors. Route to whichever member (MailerLite / OmniSend / Brevo) has headroom. When fallback fires: migrate users to next phase OR upgrade constrained provider tier.

routing_rules.label: Beehiiv prune → Phase 2
routing_rules.trigger: user received 4–7 Phase 1 emails AND beehiiv contacts > 2,000
routing_rules.notes: Move to Phase 2 paid or conversion track.

routing_rules.label: Phase 3 perpetual free
routing_rules.notes: Google sheet + Supabase email_funnel_status; 6mo inactive → delete.

routing_rules.label: Fallback triggered — ops response
routing_rules.notes: Migrate users to next funnel phase OR pay to expand the constrained primary provider tier.

routing_rules.label: Loops — marketing / product-help fallback
routing_rules.trigger: Lifecycle fallback pool active; Loops has headroom
routing_rules.notes: Exclude legal_notice and incident_notice. Powered by Loops footer on free tier.

routing_rules.label: Operational — SES primary
routing_rules.notes: Use SES for full-list blasts. Sandbox until production_pending=false; Brevo operational pool last resort only.

capacity_cliffs.action: Prune users with 4–7 Phase 1 emails; route overflow to fallback pool or Phase 2
Route to fallback member with most headroom (prefer Loops for marketing sequences); evaluate phase migration or provider upgrade
Block automated bulk; verified-email test sends only (--allow-sandbox)
Use MailerLite or Brevo for marketing fallback overflow

capacity_cliffs.trigger: Paid lifecycle daily > 80 on Resend (interim Mailgun replacement)
capacity_cliffs.action: Route to fallback pool (Brevo or Loops member)
Fallback pool; cap at 3–4 conversion emails/user; accelerate Phase 3 transfer

pseudo_buckets.label: All active users (operational)
pseudo_buckets.definition: users.status = active with valid email — synced for SES operational blasts (not nurture)

pseudo_buckets.label: Waitlist / acquisition
pseudo_buckets.definition: Pre-signup or launch-funnel contacts entering as new users (PH and future launches)

pseudo_buckets.label: Paid (Stripe)
pseudo_buckets.definition: Active Zen / $20/mo user_plans row from 2026-05-24 onward

pseudo_buckets.label: Limit hitters
pseudo_buckets.definition: Free users who hit daily token cap (cross-bucket monetization segment)

pseudo_buckets.label: Company email (B2B)
pseudo_buckets.definition: Email domain not in launch_config.consumer_email_domains — HubSpot sync + enterprise sequences

pseudo_buckets.label: Perpetual free
pseudo_buckets.definition: Phase 3 terminal — 7–11 lifecycle emails (4–7 Phase 1 + 3–4 Phase 2) with no upgrade; Google sheet + Supabase email_funnel_status; 6mo inactive → delete

funnel_phases.label: Phase 1 — Welcome / onboarding / activation
activation_goals: Welcome — orient new user to Oasis
Complete onboarding — import data from prior browser (Chrome, Safari, Brave, Edge, Firefox)
Train AI assistant at least once (feedback_events; earns 1,000 tokens)
Continue nurture on Beehiiv while capacity allows
funnel_phases.readiness_model: Graduated readiness — more milestones = riper for Phase 2. Training (feedback_events) signals deep unique-feature adoption beyond a first prompt. No single milestone triggers Phase 2 alone.
funnel_phases.prune_override: When Beehiiv nears 2,500 subs: prune users who received most Phase 1 emails and sufficient days on list, even if product milestones are incomplete, to free contacts for new signups.
exit_triggers: Graduated readiness — most milestones met (especially limit hit, training, core emails) → Phase 2 fork evaluation
Beehiiv contacts ≥2,000 — prune users with most Phase 1 emails + sufficient days, even if milestones incomplete

kpis.label: % new users who hit daily limit
Speed to first limit hit / first prompt
Median hours to first training / feedback

funnel_phases.nurture_model: Stay on Beehiiv while list headroom exists; track readiness milestones — do not cap nurture artificially at 4–7 if user has not reached strong readiness.
funnel_phases.exit: Phase 1 ends on upgrade, graduated readiness (most milestones), or Beehiiv prune — not a fixed email count or single milestone.
funnel_phases.notes: Beehiiv 2,500 subs · unlimited sends. Activation = welcome + browser import + first AI + training. Training = sticky deep-feature signal; limit hit = upgrade inflection.

funnel_phases.label: Phase 2 — Upgraded
funnel_phases.trigger: Stripe upgrade or company email on signup
funnel_phases.notes: Resend interim (100/day · 3k/mo, replaces Mailgun) + HubSpot CRM (1M contacts).

funnel_phases.label: Phase 2 — Not upgraded
funnel_phases.exit: Phase 3 perpetual free after 3–4 emails (7–11 total with Phase 1)
funnel_phases.notes: EmailOctopus 2,500 contacts · 10k/mo. Upgrade during conversion → Phase 2 paid.

funnel_phases.label: Operational — legal & incident
funnel_phases.trigger: Manual engineer broadcast — not lifecycle automation
funnel_phases.notes: All active users via operational sync. SES primary (sandbox until production). Brevo last resort in operational_pool. Not nurture.

funnel_phases.label: Phase 3 — Terminal

paths.notes: Paid user remains in HubSpot CRM
Supabase status update; 6mo inactive → account removed

funnel_phases.notes: No further lifecycle email. Paid = HubSpot. Free = sheet + Supabase flag.

fallback_pool.notes: Fallback pool — lifecycle nurture overflow only. Route to whichever member has headroom (Brevo, Loops, Resend 100/day). Never legal or incident (SES). When fallback fires: migrate users to next phase OR upgrade constrained provider tier.

operational_pool.notes: Legal, privacy, outage only. SES primary; sandbox 200/day until production. Brevo emergency last resort — separate list; do not steal CS agent daily budget without review.

scenario_presets_operational.label: Full userbase blast (current scale)
scenario_presets_operational.notes: At ~122 users, one Resend blast fits in daily cap. At 500+ users, plan multi-day sends or SES failover.
